title: Entity and Component Descriptors
text: Entity and Component Descriptors Descriptors are formal descriptions of entities or components that Uniface uses to control the interface to them. They include entity descriptors and component signatures. Descriptors are generated during compilation and stored as resource files. Entity descriptors are generated for the entities when you compile (analyze) a modeled entities. The entity descriptors are used when converting data, creating tables, importing and exporting data, and so on. The are stored as .edc resource files. Component signatures are generated when you compile components. They provide information about the component interface, such as the component name and the operations it contains. In a runtime environment, Uniface uses signatures to activate component instances and their operations. They are stored as .sig files. Related Links Signatures A Uniface signature describes the remote interface of a component. The interface enables other components to address the component without knowing anything about the implementation. This allows components of different origin to communicate through their respective interfaces. Signature Editor The Signature Editor enables you to define, edit, and compile component signatures for both Uniface and non-Uniface components. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
